title: Advocacy * Foster Children - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Advocacy * Foster Children 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Domestic Violence Issues Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients Former Foster Children Foster Children Hunger/Food Issues Sexual Assault Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON CASA NETWORK COURT APPOINTED SPECIAL ADVOCATES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, trains, and supports citizen volunteers who serve as Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASA). CASA volunteers advocate in court and in the community for the rights of children who have been abused and neglected, and are under the protection of the juvenile courts. Eligibility: Volunteers need to meet the following minimum qualifications: -Age 21 and older -Pass criminal background checks -Ability to complete and maintain training -Ability to commit to the program for at least 2 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30pm-5pm Email (971) 301-2873 Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE FOSTER CARE OMBUDSMAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves as an independent resource to investigate complaints, concerns, or violations of rights for children in the custody of ODHS Foster Care. Established through legislation developed and passed by current and former foster youth, the office receives, investigates, and helps resolve concerns from a wide range of people, including foster youth, parents, relatives, CASAs, attorneys, social workers, and other interested parties. The Ombudsman listens to concerns, documents complaints, gathers all relevant information, remains neutral and impartial, and provides guidance on how to help. Operates the Youth Empowerment and Safety (YES) line, a hotline available not only for complaints but for foster youth to ask questions about their rights, care and well-being, or any issues with their placement or services while in care. 500 Summer Street NE E-17 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (855) 840-6036 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES FOSTER CARE OMBUDSMAN
